content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Present from Lena". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/118/B
constraints: 1 <= number of tiles in each pile <= 1000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of white tiles. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the number of tiles in each pile.
content.outputFormat: The output should be a single integer, which is the minimum number of moves required to make all piles have the same number of tiles.

solution.approach: Calculate the median of the number of tiles in each pile and then calculate the total number of moves required to make all piles have the same number of tiles as the median.

code.Python3:
import sys
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
arr.sort()
median = arr[n // 2]
moves = sum(abs(x - median) for x in arr)
print(moves)
